skills.description: This skill provides executable patterns for unit testing Jakarta Bean Validation annotations and custom validators using JUnit 5. Tests run in isolation without Spring context.

skills.name: issue-fields-migration

skills.author: pbakaus

skills.name: animate
skills.description: Analyze a feature and strategically add animations and micro-interactions that enhance understanding, provide feedback, and create delight.
skills.author: pbakaus

skills.name: normalize
skills.description: Analyze and redesign the feature to perfectly match our design system standards, aesthetics, and established patterns.
skills.author: pbakaus

skills.name: quieter
skills.description: Reduce visual intensity in designs that are too bold, aggressive, or overstimulating, creating a more refined and approachable aesthetic without losing effectiveness.
skills.author: pbakaus

skills.name: clarify
skills.description: Identify and improve unclear, confusing, or poorly written interface text to make the product easier to understand and use.

skills.description: Strategically introduce color to designs that are too monochromatic, gray, or lacking in visual warmth and personality.
skills.author: pbakaus

skills.description: Invoke /impeccable — it contains design principles, anti-patterns, and the Context Gathering Protocol . Follow the protocol before proceeding — if no design context exists yet, you MUST run /impeccable teach first. Additionally gather: the "aha moment" you want users to reach, and users' experience level.

skills.name: bolder
skills.description: Increase visual impact and personality in designs that are too safe, generic, or visually underwhelming, creating more engaging and memorable experiences.
skills.author: pbakaus

skills.name: delight
skills.description: Identify opportunities to add moments of joy, personality, and unexpected polish that transform functional interfaces into delightful experiences.
skills.author: pbakaus

skills.name: extract
skills.description: Identify reusable patterns, components, and design tokens, then extract and consolidate them into the design system for systematic reuse.

skills.name: distill

skills.name: azure-rbac
skills.description: Use the 'azure__documentation' tool to find the minimal role definition that matches the desired permissions the user wants to assign to an identity. If no built-in role matches the desired permissions, use the 'azure__extension_cli_generate' tool to create a custom role definition with the desired permissions. Then use the 'azure__extension_cli_generate' tool to generate the CLI commands needed to assign that role to the identity. Finally, use the 'azure__bicepschema' and 'azure__get_azure_bestpractices' tools to provide a Bicep code snippet for adding the role assignment. If user is asking about role necessary to set access, refer to Prerequisites for Granting Roles down below:
